verification_questions.question: Does feature.md Step 3 ('Write epic state') now capture pre_existing_dirty_files by running a git status command immediately before writing epic-state.json, storing the list of files with uncommitted changes?
Does the epic-state.json template in feature.md include a pre_existing_dirty_files field (an array of file paths, defaulting to []) alongside the existing epic_name/status/phase fields?
Does the verification-examiner spawn call in feature.md Step 5.3 (lines ~697-699) pass the pre_existing_dirty_files list (read from epic-state.json) to each examiner in the spawn prompt context?
Does verification-examiner.md's Input section (lines ~11-15) declare the pre-existing dirty files baseline as an explicit input field so the examiner knows to expect and use it?

Does verification-examiner.md's scope coverage check instruct the examiner to exclude files listed in pre_existing_dirty_files when evaluating scope-boundary ACs (i.e., a file in pre_existing_dirty_files should not be flagged as a scope violation even if git diff --name-only lists it)?

Is the snapshot positioned at the correct epic lifecycle point — captured once at epic startup (Step 3), before any story work or architect runs, and not re-captured per-story — so that the baseline reflects the true pre-epic working-tree state?

Is the fix backwards-compatible: when the working tree is clean at epic start, pre_existing_dirty_files is [] (empty array, never null or absent), and the examiner's exclusion logic degrades gracefully to the existing behavior?
